english: snód (snod) milk-vessel. — ₒo-sprí ('o spri), ₒo-srí ('o sri), cream. — ₒo-már ('o mar) 1. milk and butter Sch. 2. termin. of ₒó-ma ('o ma) into the milk. — ₒo-zó ('o zo) milk-pail.
W. pater-noster pea, the seed of Abrus precatorius, used as beads for rosaries.
ₒo-yó ('o yo), ₒo-yóg ('o yog) terrier Sch.
W., only in *'ó-so taṅ-će* ('o so tang ce) or *gyáb-će* (gyab ce) to laugh at, deride, to feel a pleasure at the misfortune of others.
mulberry, ₒó-se-śiṅ ('o se shing) mulberry-tree; ba-ₒós (ba 'os) Med., perh. strawberry spinach, Blitum, which in W. is called ba-ₒ-se (ba 'o se), cow-mulberry.

W. *yog* (yog), Ts. *wag* (wag), 1. root signifying below, or with reference to time, after, opp. to goṅ (gong); ₒóg-tu ('og tu), W. *yóg-la* (yog la) 1. adv. down, below, underneath; afterwards, later; in paging books it denotes the second page of a leaf, v. goṅ (gong); it is used as an expedient to correct errors in numbering, or to make additions, as with us e.g. 'page 24, b'. 2. postp. under, with accus., less frq. with dat., down from; after (as to time, rank, succession). — ₒóg-na ('og na), W. *yóg-na* (yog na), 1. adv. underneath, below. 2. postp. c. gen. under, after. — ₒóg-nas ('og nas), W. *yóg-nas* (yog nas) 1. adv. from under, from below. 2. postp. c. genit. forth from below ₒóg-tu ǰúg-pa ('og tu 'jug pa) to put underneath, to subject, subdue Glr.; k̇a-ₒóg (kha 'og) Ts. = ₒóg-tu ('og tu), e.g. *śiṅ-gī k̇a-wág* (shing gi kha wag) under the tree; sometimes (less corr.) with accus. inst. of genit., also ₒog ('og) alone, inst. of ₒóg-tu ('og tu), ₒóg-na ('og na): *Ru-tog Gu-lab-siṅ 'og mi dug* (ru tog gu lab sing 'og mi dug) W Rutog does not stand under, is not subordinate to, Gulab Singh; ldiṅ-ₒog (lding 'og) the division of soldiers under the Dingpon, or a century (division of hundred); bču-ₒog (bcu 'og) a body of ten men under a bču-dpon (bcu dpon) or corporal. — 2. testicles, of animals, ₒog-čan ('og can) not castrated; *wog cè'-pa* (wog ce' pa) (spyad-pa (spyad pa)) to cover, copulate C. Comp. and deriv. ₒóg-sgo ('og sgo) the lower orifices of the body for the discharge of the excretions, ₒóg-sgo ṛñyis ('og sgo rnyis) S.g.; more partic. the anus Pth. — ₒog-ṛdo ('og rdo) anvil Sch. — ₒog-pag ('og pag) v. pag (pag). — ₒóg-ma ('og ma) adj. the lower, later, following one, dei ₒóg-ma (de'i 'og ma) the one following after that, the second in turn; *lá-mẹ saṅ ge-nyéṅ yóg-ma žig dug* (la me sang ge nyeng yog ma zhig dug) W. a Genyen is inferior to a Lama. ₒog-mín ('og min), अकनिष्ठ, 'the not inferiors' i.e. the highest, the inmates of a certain heaven inhabited by gods, or also that heaven itself. — ₒóg-rol-tu ('og rol tu) = ₒóg-tu ('og tu) Tar. — ₒog-rlúṅ ('og rlung) Lt. vapour, flatulence. — ₒog-śúl ('og shul) crop, craw of birds.

pf. oṅs (ongs), imp. śog (shog), B. and Bal. (*'óṅ-ċas* ('ong cas)), for which in common life almost always, and in more recent literature not seldom, yóṅ-ba (yong ba), W. *yóṅ-ċe* (yong ce), is used, 1. to come, ma ₒóṅ-ba mtóṅ-nas (ma 'ong ba mthong nas) Dzl. when he saw his mother coming; náṅ-du ₒóṅs (nang du 'ongs), Dzl. he came in; ṗyír ₒóṅ-ba (phyir 'ong ba) Glr. to come back; mi ṛnyis ṅai drúṅ-du ₒóṅ-rgyu yín-pa (mi rnyis ngai drung du 'ong rgyu yin pa) Glr. two men that were about to come to me; ₒóṅ-bai lám-du ('ong bai lam du) Pth. when being on their way; tī-se-la sgóm-du yóṅs-pa yín (ti se la sgom du yongs pa yin) Mil. we come to the Tise in order to meditate; ₒóṅs-pa légs-so ('ongs pa legs so) you are welcome Cs.; ṅas ₒo-dód byas kyaṅ ₒóṅ-mk̇an med (ngas 'o dod byas kyang 'ong mkhan med) Pth. although I was crying for help, nobody came; k̇yer ₒóṅs-so (khyer 'ongs so) Glr. they came to bring, they brought with them; k̇ríd-śog (khrid shog) bring hither! k̇rid ₒóṅs-so (khrid 'ongs so) Glr. they brought thither; with reference to time: ma-ₒóṅs-pa (ma 'ongs pa) not yet come, i.e. future, dus (dus) etc. very frq.; also poet.: ma-ₒóṅs dón-du (ma 'ongs don du) for the benefit of those that are to come, i.e. of posterity; čaṅ yóṅ-bai rigs (cang yong bai rigs), Wdṅ., the kinds (of cerealia) from which beer comes (is made). — 2. to happen, yód-pa yóṅ-gin ₒdúg-pas (yod pa yong gin 'dug pas) Mil. as it sometimes happens that there are ... ; more frq. to occur, to be met with, gṛéṅ-bu ₒóṅ gyúr-na (greng bu 'ong gyur na) whenever an e occurs, wherever an e stands Gram.; míi yul-na mi ₒóṅ (mii yul na mi 'ong) such a thing does not occur on earth Glr.; *dí-ru mi yóṅ(-ċe)* (di ru mi yong ce) W. that is not to be met with here. — 3. to fall to the lot of, to be given, to come upon, c. dat., sras ₒóṅ-bai gsól-ba btáb-bo (sras 'ong bai gsol ba btab bo) Pth. she prayed that a son might be given to her; *ko-la nad yoṅs* (ko la nad yongs) W. a disease came upon him; *sód-nyom yoṅ* (sod nyom yong) I receive alms, *sod-nam yoṅ* (sod nam yong) I acquire merit W.; to come in, yoṅ-sgo (yong sgo) income, revenue Schr., cf. yoṅ- (yong)
